title: erythrococca angolensis
intro: Erythrococca angolensis, also known as the African poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is a shrub with a toxic alkaloid compound called erythrinine, which has medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Erythrococca angolensis include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a traditional remedy for snake bites and insect stings. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by extracting the roots and using them in teas, infusions, or topical applications, although caution should be exercised due to the plant's toxic nature.
